title: Zydus Lifesciences shares in focus as firm acquires US-based Assertio Holdings for $166 million
summary: Zydus Lifesciences has successfully acquired US-based Assertio Holdings Inc. for $166.4 million in a cash tender offer. This strategic move, completed through its subsidiary Zara Merger Sub Inc., makes Assertio a wholly-owned subsidiary and strengthens Zydus's pipeline of innovative drugs, particularly in specialty and oncology supportive-care therapies.

title: Prime Focus shares in focus as SEBI halts proceedings in misleading financials case
summary: Sebi closed adjudication proceedings against Prime Focus and its directors after concluding the company applied the correct accounting treatment in business transfers to indirect subsidiaries. Allegations of misleading financial statements, accounting irregularities and regulatory violations were not substantiated, providing relief to the company and nine noticees.

title: Wipro shares in focus after unveiling Anthropic-powered AI hub in Bengaluru
summary: Wipro has launched an Applied AI Center of Excellence in Bengaluru focused on Anthropic’s Claude models to scale enterprise AI adoption. The initiative includes training 10,000 professionals, deploying specialised engineers, and integrating AI across client solutions and internal operations, reinforcing its strategy of becoming a consulting-led, AI-powered organisation.

title: Aluminium stocks slide as US-Iran deal eases supply fears, ends war-driven rally
summary: Aluminium stocks tumbled up to 5% Tuesday following an interim US-Iran deal that eased supply concerns, leading to a projected further decline in prices. Analysts anticipate near-term pressure on the sector as imports are expected to resume, eroding previous premiums enjoyed by aluminium companies.

title: Markets poised for a cautious start as GIFT Nifty ticks higher
summary: Indian markets continued their upward trend on Tuesday. The Nifty closed at 23,989. Analysts anticipate this positive momentum to persist. Improved geopolitical situations, increased foreign investor interest, and falling crude oil prices are driving the market. A potential US-Iran peace agreement is boosting global sentiment. The India VIX, a fear index, saw a decline.

title: Stocks in news: Prime Focus, Groww, GIC RE, Wipro, Nykaa
summary: Indian benchmark indices traded with a positive bias, with the Nifty consolidating near the 24,000 mark and closing near its day's high. Several companies, including Prime Focus, GIC RE, Wipro, Groww, and Nykaa, were in focus due to significant news developments. These ranged from regulatory approvals and successful acquisitions to strategic AI collaborations and expansion into international markets.

title: Bank provisioning at 3-year low on better recoveries, asset quality
summary: Banks' loan loss provisioning hit a 12-quarter low in March 2026, driven by private sector banks amid improving asset quality and recoveries. Overall provisioning dropped 23.5% year-on-year, with most banks reporting lower provisions. Public sector banks, however, saw a sequential increase in provisioning.

title: Prudent ARC top bidder for Aban Offshore debt
summary: Prudent ARC has become the highest bidder for Aban Offshore's ₹1,080 crore debt from Punjab National Bank, offering ₹193 crore. This marks the first time a bank has sold Aban Offshore's debt, resulting in an excess recovery for PNB compared to the promoters' settlement offer. The move allows PNB to avoid lengthy legal recovery processes.

title: Ola Electric files application to settle Sebi probe
summary: Ola Electric and its founder Bhavish Aggarwal have approached Sebi to settle allegations of disseminating false information to inflate stock prices. The company seeks an amicable closure to proceedings concerning misleading claims on service network expansion and sales performance, following a regulator's notice.

title: Retail players can skip GIC Re's OFS: Analysts
summary: Retail investors are advised to skip the government's offer for sale in General Insurance Corp of India. Analysts point to ongoing profitability challenges and headwinds in the reinsurance sector. The company's reliance on its investment portfolio for profits is a key concern. Despite a discount, the offer faces scrutiny.

title: Foreign investors may bet big on Indian stocks after regulatory clarity emerges
summary: India has opened its stock markets to direct investment by foreign individuals, aiming to broaden capital sources beyond FPIs. While a long-term positive, initial inflows are expected to be slow due to operational, tax, and compliance hurdles for offshore investors navigating new procedures.

title: Stock Radar: Why TMPV is a good long term buy at current levels; stock rallies over 20% in last 3 months
summary: Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles Ltd (TMPV) is showing signs of a comeback, bouncing strongly from multi-year lows around the ₹190-210 support zone. Experts suggest this could lead to a significant rally, with a target of ₹700 within the next 12 months, driven by aggressive buying at these historical support levels.

title: SC's ₹30,000 homemaker benchmark may lift accident compensation payouts
summary: A Supreme Court ruling has assigned a notional monthly value of ₹30,000 to unpaid household work. This decision is expected to significantly increase compensation payouts in motor accident claims. Insurers will likely need to reassess their reserves for third-party claims. The ruling recognizes the economic contribution of homemakers, potentially leading to higher awards in death and injury cases.

title: Areion seeks RBI approval for Rs 936 crore Aviom takeover
summary: Areion Group is seeking Reserve Bank of India approval for its acquisition of Aviom India Housing Finance. The deal, valued at ₹936 crore, is undergoing rigorous review. The RBI will closely examine the source of funds and Areion Group's governance. This assessment ensures the acquisition is backed by legitimate capital.

title: HDFC Bank board to review findings on Atanu Chakraborty's exit on June 18
summary: HDFC Bank's board meets June 18 to review legal findings on Atanu Chakraborty's concerns. Preliminary reports suggest no governance issues. The board will also seek a three-month extension for interim chairman Keki Mistry, pending RBI approval. Mistry's term ends June 18. The bank awaits the central bank's response on the extension request.

title: Vedanta Power to Vedanta Aluminium Metal: Which Vedanta demerger stock offers the best risk-reward?
summary: The restructuring of Vedanta Ltd into five distinct businesses allows investors to focus on sector-specific growth. Analysts note that while opportunities exist in aluminium and energy, each new entity faces unique risks and market challenges following the demerger.
